findings.message: The create_item mutation now accepts a non-null `description` argument (line 43) but the resolver ignores it: `repository.create_item(session, name)` is called with only `name`. Clients are forced to supply `description`, yet it is silently dropped and never persisted, so the schema advertises an input that has no effect.
findings.suggestion: Either persist the new input by threading it through the repository (e.g. `repository.create_item(session, name, description=description)` and add a `description` column/parameter), or make `description` an optional argument (`description: str | None = None`) until the persistence layer supports it, and surface it on the returned Item type so the API contract matches behavior.
